items.description: Date and time in ISO 8601 format when a submission can last be submitted by learner
URL end point for the resource. It must be present on all responses containing the resource and may be used for subsequent operations on that resource.
label to use in the Tool Consumer UI (Gradebook)
Tool resource identifier for which this line item is receiving scores from
Id of the tool platform's resource link to which this line item is attached to
Date and time in ISO 8601 format when a submission can start being submitted by learner
additional information about the line item; may be used by the tool to identify line items attached to the same resource or resource link (example: grade, originality, participation)
A simple paged collections of line items.
Comment visible to the student about the Result.
URL end point for the resource. It must be present on all responses containing the resource and may be used for subsequent operations on that resource.
Maximum possible score for this result; 1 is the default value and will be assumed if not specified otherwise.
Current score for this line item and user, in scale with resultMaximum
URL of the line item this result belongs to; must be the same as the url property of the line item.
recipient of the result, usually a student
A simple paged collections of results.
indicate to the tool platform the status of the user towards the activity's completion.
Comment visible to the student about this score.

indicate to the platform the status of the grading process, including allowing to inform when human intervention is needed. A value other than FullyGraded may cause the tool platform to ignore the scoreGiven value if present.
Current score received in the tool for this line item and user, in scale with scoreMaximum
Maximum possible score for this result; It must be present if scoreGiven is present.
Date and time when the score was modified in the tool. Should use subsecond precision.
recipient of the score, usually a student. Must be present when publishing a score update through Scores.POST operation.
restricts the maximum number of items to be returned. The response may be further constrained.

indicates the offset for which this page should start containing items. Used exclusively by the nextPage URL. For illustration purposes only. It is up to the tool platform to decide how to encode the offset in the next page URL.
limit the line items returned to only those which have been associated with the specified tool resource ID
limit the line items returned to only those which have been associated with the specified tool platform's resource link
limit the line items returned to only those which have been associated with the specified tag
limit the line items returned to only those which have been associated with this user. Results must contain at most one result.
removes the line item. While no more associated with the tool, the tool platform may decide to keep the line item around (unassociated)
returns all the line items associated to tool in the specified learning context. Results may be broken in multiple pages in particular if a limit parameter is present.
returns the current value for the line item.

returns all the results for the line item (results for all the students in the current context for this line item) Results may be broken in multiple pages in particular if a limit parameter is present.

add a new line item; the created line item is returned with an id property being the url to be used to later update, delete the item, post new scores (by appending /scores to the path), or getting the current results associated with that lineitem (by appending /results to the path).
publishes a score update. Tool platform may decide to change the result value based on the updated score.
updates the line item

conformance.statement: A tool reads the AGS endpoint claim — the lineitems URL and the scopes it was granted (lineitem, lineitem.readonly, result.readonly, score) — and restricts itself to those scopes. (The claim is an LTI launch claim, not part of the AGS OpenAPI, so it has no schema anchor here.)
A line item MUST carry a label (the gradebook-column title) and a scoreMaximum.
A line item is identified by an id URL present on all responses, and MAY bind to a resource link (resourceLinkId) and a tool resource (resourceId).
The line item container (GET /lineitems) returns a paged array of line items, filterable by resource_link_id, resource_id and tag and paged by limit/page.
A tool publishes a score by POSTing to the scores endpoint; the Score MUST carry userId, activityProgress, gradingProgress and timestamp.
When scoreGiven is present it MUST be accompanied by scoreMaximum (the scale it is given in).

The result container (GET …/results, filterable by user_id) returns results; a Result references its line item (scoreOf), the user (userId) and the achieved and maximum scores (resultScore / resultMaximum).
A tool manages line items over the full REST surface: create (POST /lineitems) and read/update/delete the single line item (GET/PUT/DELETE …/lineitems/{lineItemId}).

A tool MUST implement the OpenID Connect third-party-initiated login: on the login initiation it returns an authentication request to the platform's authorization endpoint. Every launch follows OIDC — there are no exceptions.
A tool MUST validate the id_token: a JWT signed (RS256) with the platform key resolved via its JWKS, with valid iss, aud, exp and iat, and the nonce the tool issued — rejecting any that fail.
A resource-link launch MUST carry the required claims: message_type=LtiResourceLinkRequest, version=1.3.0, the deployment_id, the target_link_uri, and the resource_link claim with an id.
The launch MUST carry the roles claim (an array of role URIs) binding the user to the context; the tool authorizes from it.
A tool MUST reject a launch missing a required claim, carrying the wrong LTI version, with an invalid iat/exp, or whose JWT signature or kid does not verify.
A LtiDeepLinkingRequest carries the deep_linking_settings claim with deep_link_return_url, accept_types and accept_presentation_document_targets.
The request lti_message_type MUST be LtiDeepLinkingRequest and the tool's reply LtiDeepLinkingResponse.
The tool returns the selection as a signed JWT carrying the content_items claim (a JSON array of the selected content items: ltiResourceLink, link, file, html or image).
After encoding the response JWT the tool MUST redirect the workflow to the deep_link_return_url via an auto-submitted form.
A tool consumes the NRPS claim (context_memberships_url + service_versions) from the launch to locate the membership service.
conformance.source: LTI NRPS 2.0 §2 (the names and role service claim) — https://www.imsglobal.org/spec/lti-nrps/v2p0

conformance.statement: A tool retrieves the membership container (media type application/vnd.ims.lti-nrps.v2.membershipcontainer+json) via GET on the context membership URL, following Link-header paging.
Each member carries a user_id (the launch sub) and a roles array; a member with status Inactive/Deleted MUST be honoured (not treated as active).
The membership request MUST be authorized by an OAuth 2.0 access token bearing the scope https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/lti-nrps/scope/contextmembership.readonly.

A proctoring tool receives the LtiStartProctoring message (with the proctoring_settings and the start_assessment_url) and only launches the assessment via the start_assessment_url once proctoring setup completes.
The tool returns control to the platform with an LtiEndAssessment message at the end of the attempt (and MAY surface an Assessment Control Service verdict during it).
All JWT signing and verification MUST use asymmetric RSA-256; symmetric cryptosystems are forbidden and a JWT MUST be signed only with the holder's private key.
All communication endpoints MUST be secured with TLS (SSL alone is forbidden).
A tool MUST expose its public keys for the platform to verify the tool's JWTs — a JWKS URL (recommended) or a registered public key.
To call a service (NRPS / AGS) a tool MUST obtain an OAuth 2.0 access token via the client-credentials grant, presenting a signed client-assertion JWT and requesting the service scope.
